content: Immediate safety: hospitalize if imminent risk (specific plan, intent, means available, unable to contract for safety). Safety planning intervention includes: recognizing warning signs, internal coping strategies, social contacts for distraction, family/friends for help, professional resources, making environment safe (means restriction). Evidence-based therapies: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) reduces attempts in BPD; Cognitive Therapy for Suicide Prevention (CT-SP); Collaborative Assessment and Management of Suicidality (CAMS). Pharmacotherapy: treat underlying disorders (lithium reduces suicide in bipolar/depression; clozapine reduces suicide in schizophrenia; ketamine shows rapid anti-suicidal effects). Means restriction counseling critical. Follow-up contact post-discharge reduces reattempts.
